title: acrocarpus fraxinifolius
intro: Acrocarpus fraxinifolius, also known as the Burmese ironwood, is a large deciduous tree species native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acrocarpus fraxinifolius include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various infections, as well as promoting digestive health and reducing inflammation. The herbal preparations of Acrocarpus fraxinifolius can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
